title: The reality of NGE
wikitext:
[]: The reality of NGE             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 1:34 pm After much watching and consideration of the Evangelion series, I've come up with an explanation for much of the show. In particular, the Alternate Reality scene from episode 26 is what gave me this idea in the first place. It's also the most controversial theory relating to the show that I've ever had, to this date. All of Neon Genesis Evangelion, up to the end of episode 24, is one of the infinite scenarios that Shinji Ikari's mind is presenting for realities that could have been. What if instrumentality happened before episode 1? What if the entire NGE universe already happened, and what was presented to us wasn't the real scenario at all? Now, I've taken into account much of the flashback episode, episode 20, and determined that all of that probably did indeed happen. Everything happened as normal up to 2015, and instrumentality occurred in that year. That would be the explanation for Shinji's self-visualization of himself as a teenager. It would also give a more concrete explanation to the scenes in Shinji's mind that take place on the orange-tinted overland train that we saw so often; it was a return to the world of instrumentality from his fake reality. It also makes possible the theory that End of Evangelion is Shinji's re-imagining of what could have been, and that the end of episode 26 happens directly before Shinji wakes up on the beach with Asuka. In short, it makes episodes 1-24 and 25' the inner ideas of Shinji's mind and NOT reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 04, 2013 2:11 pm Wha? The whole point to the series is reality can be painful but it's still worth living. For that to have any meaning we'd have to know what Shinji's reality is/was.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 3:43 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Wha? The whole point to the series is reality can be painful but it's still worth living. For that to have any meaning we'd have to know what Shinji's reality is/was.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Jan 04, 2013 3:50 pm An interesting discussion choice. Keep in mind, Nuclear Lunchbox, this isn't a debate you can't play as, "Well, you can't prove it wrong. " You will need to provide with some primary source (Examples from NGE) to back your claims, so be ready to do plenty of rewatching the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 04, 2013 3:58 pm The point was reality was painful but still worth it. To not run away. Without knowing what that reality is the revelation becomes hollow to me. I have no understanding of it. I don't know who the real Shinji is, who the real Asuka is, who the real anyone is. It just becomes empty philosophical mumbo jumbo with no context. To see how shitty his life was and watch him reject the death instinct in favor of life anyways has an impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 4:01 pm Hmm. I'm starting to have my own doubts about my theory. This is annoying. Note to self: think more carefully before posting these.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jan 04, 2013 5:15 pm If Instrumentality is exactly the same as reality to the point that no one realizes that it happened, why even bother attempting it and/or decrying it as immoral and escapist?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Jan 04, 2013 6:13 pm Everyone has hiccups, Nuclear Lunchbox. What would be foolish is defending something you no longer believe in. It creates a false sense of integrity. At the same time though, always make up your mind about something. Try not to constantly flip back and forth (or if you do, don't show it if you don't have to).

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jan 04, 2013 7:27 pm The appearance of the ghostly Rei is the strongest evidence for this line of thinking. There's a certain poetry to the idea that when Shinji and Asuka awake on the beach, it's the first time they've physically met in person and they've both been in a shared delusion. If would have to be Shinji's delusion, though, and that makes little sense. How did he get control of it? Why did he imagine all of that other stuff (episodes where he barely appears)? On the other hand, it would neatly explain why the backstory is so incoherent and technobabbly. It's not real, it's Shinji's subconscious filling in what he thinks the adults are talking about, using details from their minds. I've always flirted with the idea that there is only one Instrumentality, existing in the eleventh dimension (which contains all possible points) and the alternate reality sequence, the canon series, EoE, Rebuild, the manga, the spin-offs, all of it exist in some fashion, intersecting at a single inevitable point where they all end up the same (Instrumentality). I don't know if there's support in the text for that, though. There is a lot of indication that reality in NGE is fluid and what we see on screen is unreliable. There's a lot of unreality and dream-like ticks, like the tendency for conversations on the television to resemble the ones going on in the foreground, or the way the story starts in media res , Inception-style. (Most stories do that, though, so while it doesn't contradict, it doesn't strongly support the it's a dream/multiverse/cosmic boogey woogey interpretation either.

[]:             Quote Post by Xavierla » Wed Jan 09, 2013 8:44 pm I have a similar theory. What if everything in evangelion instead of the alternate reality seen in ep26 is all a dream and Shinji actually lives in the year 2019. I believe that the same thing occurred for Noriko from Gunbuster and everything but the ending was all a dream.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jan 09, 2013 8:59 pm I've done enough research to conclude that my own theory was complete crap. I've stopped endorsing anything along the lines of that idea.

[]:             Quote Post by hwdge » Thu Jan 10, 2013 1:51 pm its all a conspiracy theory

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:57 am It's all a feverish hallucination from Hideaki Anno's fragmented mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 11, 2013 2:51 am ^ The giant robot bits are where Shinji is having a psychotic episode, in an otherwise fairly mundane high-school setting.
summary: []: The reality of NGE             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 04, 2013 1:34 pm After much watching and consideration of the Evangelion series, I've come up with an explanation for much of the show. In particular, the Alternate Reality scene from episode 26 is what gave me this idea in the first place. It's also the most controversial theory relating to the show that I've ever had, to this date. All of Neon Genesis
